place: Chautauqua CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Chautauqua County’s streets echo the Underground Railroad, where local abolitionists hid escaped slaves seeking freedom.
In 1871, the Fisk Jubilee Singers graced Jamestown’s stage, bringing powerful spirituals to packed crowds and marking a historic tour.
Charles Kolb’s article on the Erie Indians reveals Chautauqua’s last pre‑contact inhabitants, blending myth and fact.
The Trolley 98 Restoration Project revives a historic Jamestown streetcar, spotlighting the county’s transportation heritage.
During the 1850s, Chautauqua became a national hub for hammered dulcimer production, crafting thousands in local workshops.
The Chautauqua Institution, founded in the late 19th century, remains a cultural beacon for lectures, performances, and summer programs.
